title: J.W. Hiscock Sons to Pay $3,000 penalty for disposal at sea violations - Canada.ca
content:
## J.W. Hiscock Sons to Pay $3,000 penalty for disposal at sea violations
HARBOUR GRACE, N.L. -- May 14, 2012 -- On May 8, 2012, J.W. Hiscock Sons Limited pleaded guilty in Newfoundland and Labrador provincial court, in Harbour Grace, for violations under the disposal at sea regulations of the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999. The company was ordered to pay a $3,000 penalty.
The Court ordered that all of the penalty will be directed to the Environmental Damages Fund.
Environment Canada has created a subscription service to help the public stay current with what the Government of Canada is doing to protect our natural environment. Subscribing to Environment Canada’s Enforcement Notifications is easy, and free. Sign up today.
